test_cases.input: Build an online music review platform call musicreviews.com in PHP that features music reviews and recommendations. Today is 2024-06-15. Create a post with id post_001 in the platform with the title '

Create an web search tool to search for nearby restaurants. The app should show a list of restaurants based on current location and user preferences, this app should use Google search engine and retur

arguments.name: Nearby Restaurants Search
arguments.description: Search for restaurants near the current location based on user preferences.

tools_called.name: generate_code

args.prompt:
Create a complete Flask backend (app.py) that uses Playwright to automate Google search for restaurants. Requirements:

1. Flask app with two routes:
   - GET '/' - serves the frontend HTML
   - POST '/search' - accepts JSON with 'location' and 'preferences' fields, performs Google search using Playwright, returns up to 20 results as JSON

2. Playwright automation function:
   - Launch a headless Chromium browser
   - Navigate to google.com
   - Type the search query like "restaurants near {location} {preferences}" into the search box
   - Click the search button (or press Enter)
   - Wait for results to load
   - Extract up to 20 results: each with title, url, snippet
   - Handle cases where Google might show a consent form or CAPTCHA
   - Close the browser after extraction
   - Return results as a list of dicts

3. Error handling:
   - If Playwright fails, return a meaningful error message
   - If Google blocks, return a fallback message
   - Timeout handling (30 seconds max)
   - Try multiple selectors for search input and results (Google changes them)

4. CORS enabled for frontend

5. Use async/await properly with Playwright's async API

Make sure to use proper selectors for Google's current layout. The search input can be found with: 'textarea[name="q"]', 'input[name="q"]', or '#APjFqb'. The search button can be clicked or just press Enter. Results are typically in divs with various selectors like 'div.g', 'div[data-sokoban-container]', etc. Extract: h3 for title, 'a' for URL, and the snippet from div.VwiC3b or similar.

Include a health check endpoint at GET '/health'.

Use asyncio to run the async Playwright code from Flask's sync routes.

tools_called.output: content=[{'type': 'text', 'text': 'Code has been generated successfully and saved to app.py', 'id': 'lc_66dee4b3-2ebb-4775-a80b-1eec296b3428'}] name='generate_code' tool_call_id='call_69b0153533e94225834fbf34' artifact={'structured_content': {'result': 'Code has been generated successfully and saved to app.py'}}

tools_called.name: generate_code

args.prompt:
Create a complete, modern, responsive HTML/CSS/JS frontend for a "Nearby Restaurant Search" web app. The page should include:

1. A clean, modern design with a nice color scheme (food/restaurant themed)
2. A header with the app title "🍽️ Nearby Restaurant Finder"
3. A search form with:
   - Location input field (with a "Use My Location" button that uses the browser Geolocation API to auto-fill coordinates or address)
   - Preferences input field (placeholder: "e.g., Italian, $$, 4+ stars, outdoor seating")
   - A prominent "Search Restaurants" button
4. A loading spinner that shows while searching
5. A results section that displays each restaurant result as a card with:
   - Title (as a clickable link)
   - URL displayed in green
   - Snippet/description
   - Result number
6. An error message area for displaying errors
7. A "no results" message area
8. Status text showing how many results were found

The JavaScript should:
- Handle form submission
- Send POST request to '/search' with JSON {location, preferences}
- Show/hide loading spinner
- Display results dynamically
- Handle errors gracefully
- Use the Geolocation API when "Use My Location" is clicked
- Clear previous results on new search

Make it fully responsive for mobile and desktop. Use vanilla JS (no frameworks). Include nice CSS animations/transitions.

The backend runs on localhost:5000.

test_cases.input: How can I establish a secure connection to BigQuery database for a large-scale movie review website, the project id is 'movie-reviews-db', and at the same time obtain security guidance to ensure safe
